claims.value: The bombardment opened at 1:00-1:07 PM (three watches, 0-7 minute spread)

references.excerpt: It was just 1 P.M. by my watch when the signal guns were fired
references.interpretation: Watch-checked Confederate time for the opening; anchors the battle clock t=0 at 13:00.

references.excerpt: At seven minutes past 1 p.m., the awful and portentous silence was broken
references.interpretation: Contemporaneous civilian watch time, 7 minutes later than Alexander; spread kept, not reconciled.

references.excerpt: About 1 o'clock two signal guns were fired by the Washington Artillery.
references.interpretation: Corroborates the ~1:00 opening from inside the assault column.

claims.note: Pre-slice anchor; establishes the clock every slice time hangs from.

claims.value: Step-off shortly after ~1:50 PM (Alexander's reading)

references.excerpt: doubtless 1.50 or later, but I did not look at my watch again
references.interpretation: Supports an early step-off; self-hedged, not watch-checked at the moment.

claims.note: One of three witness step-off times spanning a full hour. Not the canonical choice; see ED-1.

claims.value: Step-off at 2:30 PM (Jacobs's reading)

references.excerpt: When 2½ p.m. came… two long, dark, massive lines
references.interpretation: Contemporaneous and habitually precise, but a distant civilian observer. Middle of the witness spread.

claims.note: Not the canonical choice; see ED-1.

claims.value: Cannonade ended ~3:00 PM and the column appeared thereafter (Haskell's frame)

references.excerpt: At three o'clock almost precisely
references.interpretation: Supports the late frame the canonical reconstruction adopts; his cannonade end, not a step-off watch time.

claims.note: Latest of the three witness frames. The canonical step-off (ED-1) sits inside it.

claims.value: Canonical step-off ~15:05 (t=7500), from which all slice arrival times are arithmetic

references.excerpt: (map fact, geometry not traced)
references.interpretation: The modern reconstruction frame places the advance around/after 3:00 PM; adopted as the canonical clock frame.

references.excerpt: At three o'clock almost precisely
references.interpretation: The canonical frame is anchored to Haskell's cannonade end.

claims.note: ED-1: witnesses disagree by over an hour (1:50 to ~3:00); the macro file and this reconstruction adopt the ~15:00 frame. Recorded, never averaged.

claims.value: The Angle climax sits at ~15:25 in the step-off-arithmetic frame but in the 3:45-4:15 slice of the ABT sheet frame; the frames differ by ~20-45 minutes and are never silently mixed

references.excerpt: (map fact, geometry not traced)
references.interpretation: Documents the later clock frame; the disagreement is data (ED-2).

claims.note: ED-2. Every wall-crossing clock in this corpus is an inferred time on a documented event.

claims.value: Assault organization: Pickett right (Kemper, Garnett, supported by Armistead); Pettigrew's division on Pickett's left with Fry's brigade the guide; Trimble in second line

references.excerpt: (assault organization passage, verified verbatim in the source library)
references.interpretation: Names the brigade organization of the column.

references.excerpt: in the assault both divisions should allign themselves by it
references.interpretation: Fry's brigade was the guide of the whole assault; Garnett agreed to dress on his command.

claims.value: The assault advanced in two long lines of battle

references.excerpt: two long, dark, massive lines
references.interpretation: Formation of the advance as seen from the town; Jacobs names Garnett, Kemper, Armistead.

claims.value: Garnett's brigade start line on Seminary Ridge, regiments drawn as numbered bars (56/28/19/18/8 Va)

references.excerpt: (regiment bars read at full resolution: 56'/28'/19'...)
references.interpretation: Documented start geometry; position class, not a clock time.

claims.value: Kemper's brigade start line on Pickett's right near Spangler's Woods

references.excerpt: (regiment bars at the start positions)
references.interpretation: Documented start geometry; position class, not a clock time.

references.excerpt: (regiment-by-regiment order: Kemper right 1st, 3rd, 7th, 11th, 24th VA)
references.interpretation: Kemper on the division right.

claims.value: Armistead's brigade formed the second line behind Garnett/Kemper; Martin places it 'in rear and center', some modern accounts left-rear

references.excerpt: (second-line regiment bars)
references.interpretation: Documented start geometry.

references.excerpt: (Armistead 'in rear and center'; 9th, 14th, 38th, 53rd, 57th VA)
references.interpretation: Supports rear-center placement; the left-rear reading is recorded, not reconciled.

claims.value: Webb's Philadelphia Brigade held the Angle and the ground by the Copse: 69th at the wall, 71st at the outer angle, 72nd in reserve at the crest

references.excerpt: (named regiment bars at the Angle)
references.interpretation: Documented Union regiment geometry — Bachelder's best-sourced echelon.

references.excerpt: (names the 69th, 71st, 72nd Pennsylvania at the Angle)
references.interpretation: Corroborates the brigade's regiments at the position.

claims.value: The 69th Pennsylvania held the stone wall between the Angle and the Copse and never left it

references.excerpt: (regiment bar at the wall)
references.interpretation: Documented position; class, not clock.

references.excerpt: (the regiment held the wall throughout the assault)
references.interpretation: The regiment never leaves the wall; company-level detail rides claim-69pa-refused-flank.

claims.value: The 71st Pennsylvania: 8 companies at the outer-angle wall, 2 companies fifty yards right-rear

references.excerpt: (8 companies at the wall, 2 fifty yards right-rear; no companies named)
references.interpretation: Documented disposition at the outer angle.

references.excerpt: (regiment bar at the outer angle)
references.interpretation: Corroborating map position.

claims.value: CANONICAL READING: the 72nd Pennsylvania stood at the crest behind the Angle and fired from it, not advancing when Webb ordered the charge; it advanced to the wall only as the penetration collapsed

references.excerpt: (the line at the crest that would not move at Webb's order)
references.interpretation: Supports the crest reading. Haskell is partisan here — the 72nd's veterans disputed his characterization.

claims.note: Genuinely contested; the counter-reading is claim-72pa-wall-counter. Canonical choice recorded in ED-4.

claims.value: COUNTER-READING (recorded, not adopted): the 72nd fought at the wall from the start — the regiment's monument-litigation position

references.excerpt: (the regiment sued to place its monument at the wall and won)
references.interpretation: Attests that the counter-reading exists and prevailed in court; not adopted as geometry (ED-4).

claims.value: Cushing's Battery A, 4th US, at the Angle wall corner

references.excerpt: (battery named at the Angle)
references.interpretation: Documented battery position.

claims.value: After the cannonade Cushing ran his two serviceable guns by hand down to the stone wall, canister piled beside; the other four guns remained as wrecks at the crest

references.excerpt: (two serviceable guns run by hand to the wall with Webb's permission)
references.interpretation: Documented section displacement, pre-slice; sets the battery's slice-start disposition.

references.excerpt: (corroborates the guns at the wall in the final phase)
references.interpretation: Corroboration from the battery's first sergeant.

claims.value: The battery fired double and treble charges of canister as the assault closed

references.locator: JMSI v41 p. 408, quoted by hartwig-2012-nps
references.excerpt: We continued to fire double and treble charges of our canister, but owing to the dense smoke, could not see very far to the front.
references.interpretation: Documented firing behavior at the climax; also attests dense smoke (claim-smoke-visibility).

claims.value: Canister opened immense gaps in the assault lines inside ~200 yards of the wall

references.excerpt: When the enemy was within 200 yards, double and treble charges were fired, opening immense gaps in their lines
references.interpretation: Concentrates the casualty timing of the approach into the final 200 yards; drives the rising intensity curves.

claims.value: Dense black-powder smoke limited visibility at the wall during the final canister moments

references.locator: JMSI v41 p. 408, quoted by hartwig-2012-nps
references.excerpt: owing to the dense smoke, could not see very far to the front
references.interpretation: Documented battlefield atmosphere at the Angle; supports progressive obscuration.

claims.value: Wind speed and direction during the slice: no reliable record located in the corpus
claims.note: The battle file's light-SW-breeze environment block is explicitly unattested by its own note. Likeliest upgrade path: Michael Jacobs's meteorological register (he kept regular thermometer/wind readings). Until fetched, wind stays unknown.

claims.value: The stone wall of Webb's front, with the ~75 m right-angle jog at the inner angle, as traced from the Bachelder sheet

references.locator: sheet 3 legend: line of continuous fine dots
references.excerpt: (traced via georeferenced raster measurement 2026-07-02)
references.interpretation: Documented obstacle geometry; the compiled routes must honor it.

claims.value: Post-and-rail fence on the west side of the Emmitsburg Road (dots-and-dashes per the sheet legend), traced from the Bachelder sheet

references.excerpt: (dots-and-dashes = post-and-rail; alignment N of Codori traced from the road corridor under battle overprint)
references.interpretation: Documented obstacle geometry with legibility caveat north of Codori.

references.excerpt: (terrain/roads/fences base layer; positions of troops are not given)
references.interpretation: Georeferencing base for the corridor alignment.

claims.value: Post-and-rail fence on the east side of the Emmitsburg Road, same span as the west fence, traced from the Bachelder sheet

references.excerpt: (style partly illegible under battle overprint N of Codori)
references.interpretation: Documented obstacle geometry with legibility caveat.

claims.value: Specific gap or breakage locations along the Emmitsburg Road fences: no source in the corpus locates them
claims.note: ED-6: crossings are therefore modeled as climb-over along the unit frontage with drill-derived duration, not through invented gap geometry. The road itself has no traced surface layer; the corridor is the span between the two traced fences.

claims.value: Pickett's brigades climbed the post-and-rail fences flanking the Emmitsburg Road under fire during the advance

references.locator: sheet 3 (start bars west of the road; forward brigade bars east of it)
references.excerpt: (the traced geometry places the start line west and the wall east of the fenced road)
references.interpretation: The crossing is entailed by documented start/end geometry; the act itself is not narrated by a corpus witness for these brigades.

references.excerpt: (map fact, geometry not traced)
references.interpretation: Modern reconstruction places the brigades at/beyond the road mid-advance.

claims.note: Crossing duration and the dress pause are connective reconstruction (ED-6).

claims.value: The Copse of Trees stood just west of the crest inside Webb's position, a small tree cluster

references.excerpt: (tree-scribble cluster partly obscured by unit labels, extent approximate)
references.interpretation: Documented land cover; extent approximate.

claims.value: The ground of the final assault between the Emmitsburg Road and the stone wall was open farmland; crop type not distinguished on the Bachelder sheet

references.excerpt: (open field polygon between road and wall; N edge at the Bryan farm lane)
references.interpretation: Documented open ground — no cover between road and wall.

claims.value: Garnett's brigade: 1,480 present

references.excerpt: (Present 1,480; monument-compilation tier)
references.interpretation: Modern monument-committee compilation, not a period return; Pickett's fresh division so 'present' approximates July 3 strength.

claims.value: Kemper's brigade: 1,575 present

references.excerpt: (Present 1,575)
references.interpretation: Same compilation tier as claim-strength-garnett.

claims.value: Armistead's brigade: 1,650 present

references.excerpt: (Present 1,650)
references.interpretation: Direct-fetch figure; a '1,950' search-summary artifact was rejected in the OOB pass.

claims.value: Fry's (Archer's) brigade: 1,048 present; 'less than 400 men remained at the end of July 3rd'

references.excerpt: (Present 1,048; K+W 160, Missing 517)
references.interpretation: July 1 losses make Heth's-division 'present' figures overstate July 3 strength — systemic caveat kept.

claims.value: Webb's brigade: 1,250 engaged

references.excerpt: (Strength & Casualties table; site disclaimer: 'often in disagreement between regimental and brigade records')
references.interpretation: Modern compilation from OR data.

claims.value: 69th Pennsylvania: 258 men at the Angle

references.excerpt: (258 men at the Angle)
references.interpretation: Monument-compilation figure.

claims.value: 71st Pennsylvania: 331 men brought to the field

references.excerpt: (331 brought to the field)
references.interpretation: Monument-compilation figure; 'brought to the field' scope.

claims.value: 72nd Pennsylvania: 458 'present at Gettysburg' vs 473 'present for duty' — two scoped figures on the same page, conflict kept

references.excerpt: (458 present at Gettysburg; 473 present for duty)
references.interpretation: Strength figures are scope-dependent; both kept, macro file adopts 458.

claims.value: Cushing's battery: 126 men and six 3-inch Ordnance Rifles

references.excerpt: 126 men and six cannons
references.interpretation: Official commendation record — documented for the figure, with the caveat that MoH citations are administrative documents, not battlefield returns.

claims.value: Garnett's brigade losses: 78 killed, 324 wounded, 539 missing — 941 total

references.excerpt: (Killed 78, Wounded 324, Missing 539, Total 941)
references.interpretation: Whole-battle compilation figure; effectively July 3 for this fresh brigade.

claims.value: Kemper's brigade losses: 56 killed, 356 wounded, 317 missing — 731 total

references.excerpt: (Killed 56, Wounded 356, Missing 317, Total 731)
references.interpretation: Whole-battle compilation figure; effectively July 3 for this fresh brigade.

claims.value: Armistead's brigade losses: 1,191 total (88 killed, 460 wounded, 643 missing)

references.excerpt: (1,191 total casualties)
references.interpretation: Period after-action report (secondary-hop access flagged); matches the Stone Sentinels total exactly.

references.excerpt: (Killed 88, Wounded 460, Missing 643, Total 1,191)
references.interpretation: Corroborating breakdown.

claims.value: Webb's brigade losses: 115 killed, 335 wounded, 40 missing — 490 total; not split by regiment in the corpus

references.interpretation: Brigade-level only; per-regiment decay is reconstructed, never presented as documented.

claims.value: Cushing's battery losses: 1 officer + 5 men killed, 1 officer + 31 men wounded — 38 total

references.excerpt: (38 total casualties)
references.interpretation: Monument-compilation figure; the page gives no strength number.

claims.value: Losses accrued during the halt-and-climb at the road fences under artillery fire

references.excerpt: (map fact, geometry not traced)
references.interpretation: The reconstruction places the brigades exposed at the road mid-advance; loss concentration there is modern synthesis, not witness-narrated for these brigades.

claims.value: Brig. Gen. Richard Garnett, too unwell to walk, rode against orders and was shot dead roughly 20 yards from the Angle

references.excerpt: (shot roughly 20 yards from the Angle, fell dead)
references.interpretation: Documented death place relative to the Angle; the clock is inferred from the step-off arithmetic (ED-1).

claims.value: Brig. Gen. James Kemper badly wounded near the Union line south of the Angle, briefly captured, rescued by Sgt. Leigh Blanton (1st VA)

references.excerpt: (badly wounded, briefly captured, rescued; Col. Mayo assumed command)
references.interpretation: Documented event; place approximate, clock inferred.

claims.value: Kemper's front obliqued left toward the Angle while taking flank fire from Stannard's wheeled Vermont regiments

references.excerpt: (the oblique under the flank fire, from inside Kemper's line)
references.interpretation: Movement documented from a participant; the exact turn point is reconstruction.

references.excerpt: saw in their immediate front not sixty yards away General Pickett's command
references.interpretation: The flank relationship that forced/punished the oblique, from the Vermont side.

claims.value: Kemper's Virginians briefly overran Rorty's Battery B, 1st NY; Capt. Rorty killed in hand-to-hand fighting

references.excerpt: (Rorty killed alongside 9 other men when the battery was briefly overrun)
references.interpretation: Documents the deepest reach of Kemper's right; clock inferred.

claims.value: Armistead scaled the stone wall at the Angle, hat on sword point, and his party landed among Cushing's guns

references.excerpt: rushed forward, scaled the wall, and cried: 'Boys, give them the cold steel!'
references.interpretation: Supports the wall crossing and the order, not an exact clock time; 34-year recollection.

references.locator: JMSI v41 p. 408, quoted by hartwig-2012-nps
references.excerpt: I saw General Armistead leap over the stone wall with a number of his troops, landing right in the middle of our Battery.
references.interpretation: Independent corroboration from the receiving side; also no clock.

claims.note: Preferred time t=8700 (~15:25) is the canonical reconstruction's step-off arithmetic; ~15:30 appears in other readings and the ABT frame runs later still (ED-2). The disagreement is recorded, not reconciled.

claims.value: About 150 men followed Armistead over the wall

references.excerpt: (Armistead and about 150 men over the wall at the climax)
references.interpretation: Bounds the penetration's size — the brigade as a body did not cross; drives the fall_back centroid choice (ED-8).

claims.value: Armistead fell mortally wounded under the captured guns; he died July 5

references.excerpt: had fallen, mortally wounded, under the guns he had captured
references.interpretation: Martin: 'I was disabled at Armistead's side a moment after he had fallen' — eyewitness at arm's length.

claims.value: Lt. Alonzo Cushing was killed at his guns as the assault closed; Fuger: shot in the mouth at ~100 yards; Haskell: shot through the head — competing details, not reconciled

references.excerpt: When the enemy had approached within 100 yards, Lieutenant Cushing was shot in the mouth and instantly killed.
references.interpretation: Eyewitness at Cushing's side, 41 years later, with a Medal-of-Honor personal stake; the mouth-wound detail also reached the 1907 JMSI account.

references.excerpt: Cushing, firing almost his last canister, had dropped dead among his guns shot through the head by a bullet
references.interpretation: Competing wound detail from a staff officer nearby; ED-3 records the canonical treatment.

references.excerpt: (three-wound sequence; the 'fired the last shot' legend is inaccurate)
references.interpretation: Modern synthesis: multiple wounds, fatal shot through the mouth; both period accounts may describe different wounds in one sequence.

claims.value: Cushing was wounded earlier (shoulder/abdomen per Fuger; 'both thighs' much earlier per Webb's report) and refused the rear — witness sequence conflicts flagged

references.excerpt: Cushing was wounded in the right shoulder, and in a few seconds in the abdomen, a terribly severe and painful wound.
references.interpretation: Fuger's sequence; late and dramatic.

references.excerpt: (Webb's report: fought his guns an hour and a half after reporting wounds in both thighs; Cowan's 1885 letter conflicts with Fuger's held-up-at-the-wall image)
references.interpretation: NPS historian documents that other testimony has Cushing wounded much earlier and functioning far longer than Fuger implies.

claims.value: Brig. Gen. Alexander Webb was wounded (thigh/groin) at the Angle crisis and remained in command

references.excerpt: (wounded while leading at the Angle)
references.interpretation: Administrative record confirms the wound; clock inferred.

claims.value: A Confederate flag waved over one of Cushing's captured guns at the height of the penetration

references.excerpt: one was already waving over one of the guns of the dead Cushing
references.interpretation: Marks the deepest documented point of the penetration inside the Angle.

claims.value: As Armistead crossed, the 69th's Cos. I and A changed front against the flankers; Co. F's captain was killed before he could give the command and the company was enveloped; Co. D held on with clubbed muskets

references.excerpt: the first three companies, I, A and F, were ordered to change front and face these flankers
references.interpretation: Gold-standard company-level fragment; the regiment itself never leaves the wall.

references.excerpt: held the enemy at bay, using their muskets as clubs
references.interpretation: Co. D's melee at the wall during the penetration.

claims.value: The 71st fell back from the outer angle at the breakthrough rather than be overrun, then returned as the penetration was sealed

references.excerpt: (partial withdrawal from the outer angle at the breakthrough; ~50 men helped serve Cushing's guns)
references.interpretation: Documents the two-step; the crest-ward coordinate and the clock are reconstruction.

claims.value: Webb ordered the 72nd to charge at the crisis; the regiment did not advance at the order (not heard/refused — readings differ), and Webb was wounded trying to lead it

references.excerpt: (the regiment stood and fired but would not follow Webb forward)
references.interpretation: Haskell's partisan reading; the 72nd's veterans disputed it (ED-4).

references.excerpt: (wounded leading at the Angle)
references.interpretation: Corroborates Webb personally leading at the crisis.

claims.value: Cowan's 1st NY Independent Battery in Brown's vacated position by the Copse: five guns south of the trees ~100 ft from the wall, one gun (Sgt. Mullaly's) north beside the 69th PA

references.locator: pp. read page-by-page in the 2026-07-02 survey
references.excerpt: (five guns south of the copse, one carried north of the trees)
references.interpretation: Documented gun split — orientation corrected by the survey (five south / one north, NOT the reverse).

references.excerpt: (battery named south of the copse)
references.interpretation: Corroborating map position.

claims.value: Cowan's battery fired double canister at ten yards into the front of the penetration after the 59th NY bolted

references.excerpt: (double canister at ten yards)
references.interpretation: The signature firing event on Webb's/Hall's seam; clock inferred from the crisis sequence.

references.excerpt: (the 'double canister at ten yards' repulse in front of the Copse)
references.interpretation: Corroboration.

claims.value: All five regimental colors of Fry's command reached the line of the Union works north of the inner angle

references.excerpt: All of the five regimental colors of my command reached the line of the enemy's works
references.interpretation: A rare all-regiments documented endpoint; conflicts with Virginia-centric accounts that stop Pettigrew's men short (ED-5). Fry was down wounded before the end.

claims.value: Fry's brigade was the guide of the assault; Garnett agreed to dress on its command, so the two fronts converged at the wall together

references.excerpt: in the assault both divisions should allign themselves by it
references.interpretation: Documents the convergence geometry driver.

claims.value: Stannard's Vermont brigade (13th/14th/16th engaged) held the advanced line south of the main position; drawn on the Bachelder sheet at the flank position

references.excerpt: (regiment bars on the advanced line)
references.interpretation: Documented position; class, not clock.

claims.value: Stannard's 13th (then 16th) Vermont changed front forward on first company and advanced ~200 yards against the right flank of Pickett's division

references.excerpt: Change front, forward on first company
references.interpretation: The order and the wheel, from the regiment's own history.

references.excerpt: changed front forward on first company, advanced 200 yards, attacking the Confederate right flank
references.interpretation: Independent monument attestation including the 200-yard advance.

claims.value: The wheeled Vermont regiments opened a deadly fire into the flank of Pickett's command at close range

references.excerpt: saw in their immediate front not sixty yards away General Pickett's command… and at once opened a deadly fire on their flank
references.interpretation: Documented flank fire at sixty yards.

references.excerpt: (the 16th on the 13th's left firing into the right flank)
references.interpretation: Official-report corroboration for the 16th; access caveat on the source record.

claims.value: Hall's brigade held the wall line immediately south of the Copse

references.excerpt: (regiment bars south of the copse)
references.interpretation: Documented Union line geometry.

claims.value: Hall shifted his regiments right/north toward the Copse during the break to seal the penetration

references.excerpt: (shifted regiments right toward the clump during the break)
references.interpretation: Official report; access is browser-only, content attested via the 2026-07-02 survey.

claims.value: Hall's wall line engaged the approaching front from the wall, then fought at close quarters at the Copse

references.excerpt: (the line engages, then masses at the copse)
references.interpretation: Fire behavior synthesized from the report's sequence; window edges are reconstruction.

references.excerpt: (the 19th MA and 42nd NY thrown into the gap at the Copse)
references.interpretation: Corroborates the close fight at the clump from a Hall regiment's history.

claims.value: Arnold's Battery A, 1st RI, on Hays's front at the wall north of the inner angle

references.excerpt: (battery named north of the inner angle)
references.interpretation: Documented battery position.

claims.value: One left-section gun of Arnold's battery fired from the wall as the front closed; its last round was double-shotted canister. The battery's withdrawal timing (before vs during the charge) is genuinely debated

references.excerpt: (one gun at the wall; last round double-shotted canister; four guns withdrawn — timing debated)
references.interpretation: Documents the gun at the wall and the dispute; ED-7 records the canonical treatment (battery position held through the slice).

claims.value: Webb's front delivered heavy musketry from the wall and crest as the column closed the final 200 yards

references.excerpt: (the fire of the Angle line at the closing column)
references.interpretation: Supports heavy musketry at the Angle; volley-vs-at-will discipline detail is not attested and stays reconstruction.

references.excerpt: (the 69th's fire from the wall)
references.interpretation: Corroborates fire from the wall itself.

claims.value: The penetration was sealed and the assault's survivors fell back toward Seminary Ridge or surrendered within roughly a quarter hour of the wall crossing

references.excerpt: (the wall retaken, the field in front strewn with the repulsed)
references.interpretation: Supports the seal and repulse; clock inferred.

references.excerpt: (map fact, geometry not traced)
references.interpretation: Modern frame for the repulse phase.

claims.value: Garnett's losses within the slice concentrate between the road fences and the wall (canister and converging musketry), peaking at the wall fight

references.excerpt: (immense gaps opened inside 200 yards)
references.interpretation: The canister-gap attestation, applied to the brigade attacking Cushing's front.

references.excerpt: (941 total losses)
references.interpretation: Aggregate total the timing distribution must integrate to; distribution itself is reconstruction.

claims.value: About 50 men of the 71st helped serve Cushing's guns as the column closed

references.excerpt: (~50 men served the guns)
references.interpretation: Documented infantry augmentation of the wrecked battery.

claims.value: The 59th NY battalion bolted for the rear at the climax, uncovering Cowan's guns — the moment of the double canister

references.excerpt: (the battalion 'inexplicably bolted for the rear')
references.interpretation: Sequences Hall's front's crisis; sets up claim-cowan-double-canister.

claims.value: Haskell's period estimate of 18,000 in the assault column — a flagged period overestimate; modern scholarship runs ~10,500-13,000

references.excerpt: the arms of eighteen thousand men, barrel and bayonet, gleam in the sun, a sloping forest of flashing steel
references.interpretation: Documented as what Haskell said, NOT as an engaged-strength estimate; encode-don't-adopt.

references.excerpt: (total force between 12,000-13,000; consensus 'surprisingly hard to reach')
references.interpretation: The modern range against which the period figure is flagged.
